publicationstmt.publisher: Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library

controlnote.p: This finding aid, which is compliant with the Yale EAD Best Practice Guidelines, Version 1.0., has a MARC record in Yale's ILS with the following bib number: 3948373.

maintenanceagency.agencyname: Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library

conventiondeclaration.abbr: beinecke manuscript unit archival processing manual
conventiondeclaration.citation: translation missing: en.enumerations.resource_finding_aid_description_rules.beinecke manuscript unit archival processing manual

maintenanceevent.eventdescription: This finding aid was produced using ArchivesSpace on Wednesday September 2, 2020 at 05:31

did.unittitle: Hill papers

corpname.part: Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library

did.abstract: Letters and documents concerning Hill's activities as ambassador to the Hague and envoy to the Savoy. Also includes letters from James Bateman regarding financial matters and political appointments; from James Cressett regarding Cressett's work as a diplomat; from George Dolman regarding the naval maneuvers of the frigate, Lyme, of which Dolman was captain; and from Richard Powys regarding the passing of Hill's bills at the Treasury.

acqinfo.head: Immediate Source of Acquisition
acqinfo.p: Gift of James Marshall and Marie-Louise Osborn.

userestrict.head: Conditions Governing Use
userestrict.p: The Hill Papers are the physical property of the Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library, Yale University. Literary rights, including copyright, belong to the authors or their legal heirs and assigns. For further information, consult the appropriate curator.

prefercite.head: Preferred Citation
prefercite.p: Hill Papers. James Marshall and Marie-Louise Osborn Collection, Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library.

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: The materials are open for research.

processinfo.head: Processing Information
processinfo.p: This finding aid was produced from a previously existing card set in the Manuscripts Catalog, or from another inventory. All pertinent bibliographical information has been retained. If the collection also contains unlisted or unsorted material, this has been noted in the box-and-folder list.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This finding aid encompasses accessions of materials pertaining to Richard Hill.

did.unittitle: Letters and Documents
Anonymous. Account of the Siege of the Gescentin by the French, [n.p.]
Araciete, Marcos de. 2 ALS to Richard Hill, [n.p.]
Aremberg, Leopold, Duke of, d. 1754. ALS (in French) to Richard Hill, [n.p.]
Bateman, Sir James, d. 1718
40 ALS and 2 letters (copies) to Richard Hill, Antwerp, London and Helvoetflus

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Extensive correspondence on Hill's financial affairs, revealing the interaction between the Bank of England with the Exchequer, Lords of the Treasury, and House of Lords.

did.unittitle: 8 ALS and 2 letters (copies) to Richard Hill, London

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Personal correspondence about financial matters and political appointments.

did.unittitle: Blackwell, L. ALS to [Richard Hill], Genoa.
Caracciolo, Antonio. ALS (in Italian) to Richard Hill, [n.p.]
Cressett, James, 1655?-1710
25 ALS and 1 letter (dictated) to Richard Hill, Hamburg, Hanover, and Zell

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Humorously reveals the lifestyle, disappointments and financial difficulties of an English diplomat.

did.unittitle: 2 ALS to [Richard Powys], Cheam
D'Aversberg, Count. ALS (in French) to Richard Hill, [Turin]

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Printed in Blackley's Correspondence of Hill , I. 40.

did.unittitle: Dolman, George. 4 ALS, 3 LS and 1 letter (copy) to Richard Hill, on board the Lyme

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Reports of naval maneuvers from the British frigate, "Lyme," of which Dolman was captain.

did.unittitle: Frise, Mr. 4 ALS (in French) to Richard Hill, various places

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: With references to Mr. Blathwayt.

did.unittitle: Goetz, Baron. Letter (copy) to William Blathwayt, The Hague
Hamilton, Hans, 1652?-. Receipts, signed, Genoa

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Acknowledges receipt of money from Richard Hill for Catalonian Campaign. See Blackley's Correspondence of Hill , II. 688-690.

did.unittitle: Hill, Richard, 1655-1727
AL (draft, in French) to the Dowager-Duchess of Savoy, [n.p.]

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Sends respects from William III. Printed in Blackley's Correspondence of Hill , II. 803.

did.unittitle: AL (draft, in French) to the Duke of Savoy, [n.p.]
AL (draft) to M. Vicetti, Genoa

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Complains about an English sailor being put into chains. Vicetti was Secretary of State of Genoa. Printed in Blackley's Correspondence of Hill , II. 677 and (translation) II. 912. Accompanied by copy of anonymous letter on same subject, dated 1705 May 4/15.

did.unittitle: Paper (holograph manuscript, draft) given in by Mr. Hill to the Lord Treasurer, [n.p.]

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Report on the war in Italy. Printed in Blackley's Correspondence of Hill .

did.unittitle: Hop, Mr. Letter (copy, in French) to Lord Villiers, Amsterdam

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: On payments from the Bank of England.

did.unittitle: Jeanne de Savoie-Nemours, Duchesse. Letter (copy) to Queen Anne, Turin

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Welcomes Hill as the Queen's envoy. Printed in Blackley's Correspondence of Hill , I. 237.

did.unittitle: Karl VI, Emperor of Germany, 1685- 1740. LS (fragment, in French) to Richard Hill, Barcelona

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Signature remains, but serious loss of text. Printed in full in Blackley's Correspondence of Hill , I. 229 and (translation) II. 875.

did.unittitle: Letters and Papers concerning the War in Flanders.
Marlborough, John Churchill, 1st Duke, 1650-1722. LS (fragment, in French) to Richard Hill, from a camp near Landau

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Signature remains, but serious loss of text. Printed in full in Blackley's Correspondence of Hill , I. 169 and (translation) II. 872.

did.unittitle: Mornay, Mr. 2 ALS (in French) to [Richard Hill?], London
Netherlands (United Provinces, 1581- 1795) Staten Generaal. Extract from the Register of Resolutions of the States General

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Anonymous manuscript (in French). Instructions for Major-General Belcastel; Richard Hill's role in levying 3000 men for protection of Cevennes. Printed in Blackley's Correspondence of Hill , p. 390, and translated on p. 884.

did.unittitle: Powys, Richard, d. 1724. 16 ALS to Richard Hill, Whitehall

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: About the passing of Hill's bills at the Treasury while Powys was Paymaster. Matthew Prior is mentioned (1696/7 Feb 9 and Mar 9); Marlborough's victory is reported (1704 Aug 18).

did.unittitle: Shaw, William. 2 ALS to Richard Hill, [n.p.]

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Fragile, with some loss of text in both letters.

did.unittitle: Stepney, George, 1663-1707. ALS to Richard Hill, Potsdam

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Mentions Matthew Prior.

did.unittitle: Tucker, John
5 ALS to Richard Hill, Whitehall and Cockpit

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Printed in Blackley's Correspondence of Hill . Tucker was an Under-Secretary of State in 1707.

did.unittitle: 2 ALS to John Chetwynd, Whitehall and Cockpit

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Chetwynd was Richard Hill's secretary at the Court of Savoy; later he was on the Board of Trade.

did.unittitle: William III, King of Gt.Brit., 1650- 1702
Letter (copy, in Latin) to the Duke of Savoy, Kensington
Letter (copy, in French) to the Dowager-Duchess of Savoy, Kensington

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Printed in Blackley's Correspondence of Hill , II. 800.

did.unittitle: Letter (copy, in Latin) to the Duke of Savoy, Kensington

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Credentials for Richard Hill. Printed in Blackley's Correspondence of Hill , II. 799-800.

did.unittitle: Copies of letters (partly in cipher) addressed to [Mons. le duc Fengilea]
Letters and fragments of letters to unidentified correspondents
